learnerValue: Builds precise recall of the equal-magnitude, opposite-direction force pair in Newton’s Third Law.

issues: Choices c and d express the same underlying misconception: both make stopping distance independent of speed when braking force is fixed. The four-choice card therefore lacks three genuinely distinct distractors.
learnerValue: Tests the supported relationship between speed and stopping distance at a given braking force.
Reinforces that stopping distance combines thinking distance and braking distance.
Distinguishes driver reaction factors from road and vehicle factors affecting braking.
Connects adverse road and weather conditions and poor brakes or tyres with braking distance.
Tests both linked changes during braking: reduced kinetic energy and increased brake temperature.

issues: The official source places 6.5.5 Momentum under “HT only”. Because the target set includes a Foundation offering, this card is out of scope.
learnerValue: Accurately tests the momentum equation for Higher-tier learners.

issues: The official source places 6.5.5 Momentum under “HT only”. Because the target set includes a Foundation offering, this card is out of scope.
learnerValue: Clearly distinguishes conservation of total momentum from common Higher-tier misconceptions.
Separates frequency from amplitude, wavelength and period using their defining properties.
Builds accurate recall of the electromagnetic spectrum from long to short wavelength.
Identifies the dependent relationship investigated in required practical activity 21.
Links X-rays and gamma rays specifically to ionisation, gene mutation and cancer.
Tests the distinctive pairing of microwaves with satellite communications and cooking.
Contrasts permanent and induced magnetism, including what happens after the external field is removed.
Builds exact AQA recall of an electromagnet as a solenoid with an iron core.
